pi_items.source: Multiple sources including MedlinePlus, NCI, Cancer Research UK, and Mayo Clinic confirm Abiraterone (also known as Abiraterone Acetate or Zytiga) is a real cancer drug used to treat metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer

DrugBank, Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, Wikipedia, and other medical sources confirm Acenocoumarol is a real medication - an anticoagulant also known as Sintrom used to prevent thromboembolic diseases

Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions database (https://cancer-druginteractions.org/interactions/695/all) accessed and confirmed. The interaction summary explicitly states 'No Interaction Expected' for the Abiraterone and Acenocoumarol combination

Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions uses a Traffic Light system where green indicates 'No clinically significant interaction expected'. This is confirmed in the University of Liverpool's documentation of the Cancer Drug Interactions system. The Abiraterone-Acenocoumarol interaction with 'No Interaction Expected' status corresponds to green in this system

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database (https://cancer-druginteractions.org/). Abiraterone is a real prostate cancer medication. Acenocoumarol is a real anticoagulant medication. According to the Liverpool Cancer Drug Interactions Database, the interaction between Abiraterone and Acenocoumarol shows 'No Interaction Expected' with green color in the Traffic Light system, indicating no clinically significant interaction is expected.
